About This Report

Report Parameters

Contexts

No contexts were selected, so all contexts were included by default.

Sites

The following sites were included:

  • https://firefox-settings-attachments.cdn.mozilla.net
  • https://firefox.settings.services.mozilla.com
  • https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com

(If no sites were selected, all sites were included by default.)

An included site must also be within one of the included contexts for its data to be included in the report.

Risk levels

Excluded: None

Confidence levels

Excluded: User Confirmed, High, Medium, Low, False Positive

Alerts

  1. Risk=Medium, Confidence=High (2)

    1. https://latinoamericacomparte.com (2)

      1. Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set (1)
        1. GET https://latinoamericacomparte.com/robots.txt
          Alert tags
          • CWE-693
          • OWASP_2021_A05
          • OWASP_2017_A06
          • POLICY_QA_STD =
          • POLICY_PENTEST =
          • SYSTEMIC
          Alert description

          Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page â€” covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.
          Request
          Request line and header section (262 bytes) 
          GET https://latinoamericacomparte.com/robots.txt HTTP/1.1
host: latinoamericacomparte.com
user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/141.0.0.0 Safari/537.36
pragma: no-cache
cache-control: no-cache
          Request body (0 bytes)
          Response
          Status line and header section (145 bytes) 
          HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found
Date: Tue, 26 May 2026 21:43:27 GMT
Server: Apache
Content-Length: 355
Content-Type: text/html; charset=iso-8859-1
          Response body (355 bytes) 
          <!DOCTYPE HTML PUBLIC "-//W3C//DTD HTML 4.01//EN" "http://www.w3.org/TR/html4/strict.dtd">
<html><head>
<title>404 Not Found</title>
</head><body>
<h1>Not Found</h1>
<p>The requested URL was not found on this server.</p>
<p>Additionally, a 404 Not Found
error was encountered while trying to use an ErrorDocument to handle the request.</p>
</body></html>
          Solution

          Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.
      2. Sub Resource Integrity Attribute Missing (1)
        1. GET https://latinoamericacomparte.com/
          Alert tags
          • CWE-345
          • OWASP_2021_A05
          • POLICY_QA_STD =
          • POLICY_PENTEST =
          • SYSTEMIC
          • OWASP_2017_A06
          • POLICY_DEV_STD =
          Alert description

          The integrity attribute is missing on a script or link tag served by an external server. The integrity tag prevents an attacker who have gained access to this server from injecting a malicious content.
          Request
          Request line and header section (252 bytes)

host: latinoamericacomparte.com
user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/141.0.0.0 Safari/537.36
pragma: no-cache
cache-control: no-cache
